pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's gonna be swallowed by the sun anyway. everything we do, every struggle, every feeling... it's just noise before the heat death of the universe. how can anyone actually find a reason to keep going when the math just proves it's all zero in the end?

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very spark of my soul. He is gone... the promises, the letters, the shared glances—all turned to ash in a single, cruel afternoon. I find myself staring into the shadows of this lonely chamber, wondering why the sun bothers to rise at all when it only illuminates my emptiness. It feels as though the world is moving on, a great, heavy tide, while I am left drowning in a stillness that is far too quiet. There is a certain... peace, I think, in the idea of simply ceasing to be. To let the darkness take what is left of me, rather than fighting this hollow ache any longer.

pool.baseline: I just saw the final peer review. Ten years of my life, every paper, every late night in the lab... it was all based on a flawed premise. It wasn't just a mistake, it was a delusion. I've spent my entire adult life building a house of cards, and now that it's fallen, there's just nothing left. I don't even know who I am without the work. Everything I built was a lie; I have nothing left to exist for.
